articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Attacks in Red Sea Send Oil Prices Over $100 a Barrel
articles.description: The price of Brent crude soared after Iran’s Houthi allies in Yemen claimed strikes on two Saudi tankers. The House cast a second vote to rein in President Trump’s war powers, with four Republicans joining Democrats to rebuke the president.

articles.title: House Again Rebukes Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
articles.description: For the second time, a few Republicans joined Democrats to back a measure calling on the president to seek authorization for the conflict.

related.title: 4 House Republicans rebel against Trump, vote with Dems to block Iran war powers
related.description: House Democrats passed an Iran war powers resolution halting Trump's war in Iran with support from several GOP lawmakers.
related.normalizedTitle: 4 house republicans rebel against trump vote with dems to block iran war powers

related.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
related.description: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.
related.normalizedTitle: house adopts 95 billion g o p budget for iran war

related.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
related.description: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.
related.normalizedTitle: house passes defense bill amid iran war divide

articles.title: MORNING GLORY: President Trump, Iran and Napoleon’s still-relevant maxim
articles.description: Marco Rubio calls Iran's leaders crazy as Trump faces pressure to finish the battle against a regime driven by end-times theology and nuclear aims.

related.title: How Trump Is Getting Richer as President
related.description: Since he came back to office, President Trump has made huge amounts of money from cryptocurrency and foreign investments, posing the question of whether he is using his power as president to enrich himself. Andrea Fuller, a Times data journalist, explains.
related.normalizedTitle: how trump is getting richer as president

articles.title: U.S. oil tops $90, Brent above $98 after tankers struck off Saudi Arabia
articles.description: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

articles.title: As Part of Nuclear Deal, Trump Demands Saudi Arabia Join the Abraham Accords
articles.description: The president’s new condition could create a roadblock to the agreement his administration announced this week.

articles.title: James Woolsey, Embattled C.I.A. Director Under Clinton, Dies at 84
articles.description: He arrived in Washington a conservative Democrat and drifted rightward, supporting the 2003 invasion of Iraq and Donald J. Trump’s 2016 presidential bid.

articles.title: Zelensky’s Dizzying U-Turn Is the Latest Zigzag in a Presidency Full of Them
articles.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has pivoted from unpopular moves before, but a recent decision about military leadership and strategy could weaken his position.

articles.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
articles.description: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

articles.title: Global Oil Price Hits $100 a Barrel for First Time Since May
articles.description: The recent rebound reflects traders’ growing concern about the expanding war, which now extends from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea.

articles.title: For South Korea, U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Exposes a Double Standard
articles.description: President Trump has put Saudi Arabia on the path to enriching uranium on its own soil, a demand South Korea has been making for years.

articles.title: Even as War Rages, Investors Bet Oil Will Be Cheaper in a Few Months
articles.description: Futures contracts reflect an expectation that low inventories and midterm elections are likely to push the Trump administration to try to ease tensions with Iran.

articles.title: Traders see September rate hike as European Central Bank mulls energy price spike
articles.description: ECB president Christine Lagarde said the bank anticipates inflation to remain "well above target" until the first half of 2027.

articles.title: Edward O’Keefe Named C.E.O. of WNET
articles.description: Mr. O’Keefe, who was previously an executive at CNN, is the chief executive of the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library.

articles.title: Brent crude tops $100 a barrel. How the next stop could be $120
articles.description: Goldman Sachs warns on oil prices and so called tank bottoms ( and loves a new natural gas-related name).

articles.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
articles.description: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

related.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal contingent on relations with Israel, does not include enrichment
related.description: The civilian nuclear cooperation agreement, signed Wednesday, will be sent to Congress for review, the Department of Energy said.
related.normalizedTitle: trump says saudi nuclear deal contingent on relations with israel does not include enrichment

articles.title: SEN RAND PAUL: DC's Russia sanctions bill won't stop Putin — but it will hurt you
articles.description: This new Russia sanctions bill could impose 500% tariffs and cost Americans half a trillion dollars, targeting 40% of the world's population.

articles.title: Trump Prepares Yet Another Strategy to Resurrect Global Tariffs
articles.description: The administration’s 10 percent global tariffs are set to expire at midnight, but officials have been preparing replacements.

articles.title: Supreme Court Turbocharges Presidential Power
articles.description: A pair of decisions reshaped the structure of the federal government.

articles.title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It Wants to Go Nuclear.
articles.description: Opponents warn that a U.S.-Saudi deal could be a step toward the kingdom building an atomic bomb. But there are other reasons for the world’s largest oil exporter to diversify its energy mix.

articles.title: F.D.A. Announces New Cyclosporiasis Outbreak With 72 Cases
articles.description: Health officials did not identify the location of the outbreak, or what they believed was causing it.

articles.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
articles.description: China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.

articles.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
articles.description: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

articles.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Global Nuclear Arms Race
articles.description: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.

articles.title: Israel Welcomes Trump’s Demand for Diplomatic Ties With Saudi Arabia
articles.description: Israeli officials greeted the possibility of normalized ties, but analysts said the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal nonetheless remained bad for Israel. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has not yet commented on the agreement.

articles.title: Debate Over Ancient Iranian Jewelry Worn by Zendaya to ‘Odyssey’ Event
articles.description: The actress wore earrings to an event for “The Odyssey” that a London gallery has said were made from an ancient artifact.

articles.title: Ford Will Use Apple Maps in New Self-Driving System
articles.description: In a first, Apple software will influence the way a new line of electric vehicles made by Ford Motor operates, and other automakers may follow.

articles.title: Mamdani Knew He Couldn’t Arrest Netanyahu. But He Could Denounce Him.
articles.description: In a video message, the mayor of New York City said he could not legally arrest Israel’s prime minister but reinforced his position as a voice of the international left.

articles.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
articles.description: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

articles.title: Middle East Oil Flows Dwindle as Conflict With Iran Deepens
articles.description: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
articles.description: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

articles.title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
articles.description: At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.

related.title: Google slapped with $1 billion fine under landmark EU digital law
related.description: European regulators fined Google 890 million euros alleging the company gives preferential treatment to its own services.

articles.title: GOP firebrand moves to dissolve Congressional Black Caucus: ‘Federally sanctioned segregation’
articles.description: Nancy Mace's resolution would bar race-based House caucuses, effectively forcing dissolution of the Congressional Black Caucus and similar groups.

articles.title: FCC chair suggests ABC skipping Trump's speech will factor in license renewal decision
articles.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr suggested ABC's decision to skip President Donald Trump's primetime address could factor into the network's broadcast license renewals.

articles.title: Capitol Hill sex scandals trigger new push to redraw the line for lawmakers
articles.description: Speaker Mike Johnson endorsed new proposals banning lawmakers from dating congressional staff as multiple sexual misconduct cases shake Capitol Hill.

articles.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
articles.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

articles.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
articles.description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

articles.title: WATCH: Brandon Gill corners law school gatekeeper with DEI question she won't answer
articles.description: Rep. Brandon Gill pressed ABA president on whether diversity principles would justify rejecting a third Black Supreme Court justice nomination.

articles.title: Darline Graham reveals what Trump told her inside Oval Office after brother Lindsey's death
articles.description: Darline Graham says she used her Oval Office meeting with Trump to bring up Russia sanctions, honoring her late brother Lindsey Graham's Senate legacy.

articles.title: Cyclospora outbreak tests RFK Jr.'s promise to overhaul food system, rebuild trust in CDC
articles.description: Cyclospora presents a test for HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s promise to overhaul the food system and restore confidence in the agencies overseeing it.

articles.title: Hasan Piker makes AOC his 2028 pick for president, says it's 'not even a question’
articles.description: Hasan Piker chose AOC over Newsom, Buttigieg, and other Democrats in a 2028 presidential exercise, calling her even better than Bernie Sanders.

articles.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano may have misled Congress, Democrats say
articles.description: The IRS alone hemorrhaged more than 31,000 employees as of January in President Trump's government cuts, according to the agency's inspector general.

articles.title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
articles.description: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”

articles.title: Four Young Mathematicians Receive Prestigious Fields Medals
articles.description: The awards are bestowed only once every four years to mathematicians under the age of 40.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will hold Iran responsible for Houthi attacks after oil tankers targeted in Red Sea
articles.description: Yemen's Houthis said they had targeted two Saudi oil tankers with drones and missiles for allegedly violating its maritime blockade.

articles.title: Protesters gather in Madison after man fatally shot by a police officer
articles.description: A graphic video of the shooting during an attempted arrest in the Marquette neighborhood of Madison, Wisconsin, was quickly spread online.
